revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''鬼の岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[古墳]]の名称。巨石を使った古墳...'

revision.text:
'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[古墳]]の名称。巨石を使った古墳に対して名づけられることが多い。
* [[鬼の窟古墳 (長崎県壱岐市)]] - 長崎県壱岐市（旧芦辺町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * [[高下古墳]] - 長崎県雲仙市（旧国見町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも言う。 * [[鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県)]] - 熊本県八代市にある古墳。八代市指定史跡。 * [[鬼ノ岩屋古墳]] - 大分県別府市にある古墳。国指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * 鬼の窟古墳 - 宮崎県西都市の[[西都原古墳群]]中の古墳。西都原古墳群は国指定特別史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[古墳]]の名称。巨石を使った古墳に対して名づけられることが多い。
* [[鬼の窟古墳 (長崎県壱岐市)]] - 長崎県壱岐市（旧芦辺町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * [[高下古墳]] - 長崎県雲仙市（旧国見町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも言う。 * [[鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県)]] - 熊本県八代市にある古墳。八代市指定史跡。 * [[鬼ノ岩屋古墳]] - 大分県別府市にある古墳。国指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * 鬼の窟古墳 - 宮崎県西都市の[[西都原古墳群]]中の古墳。西都原古墳群は国指定特別史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の古墳|おにのいわや こふん]]

{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[古墳]]の名称。巨石を使った古墳に対して名づけられることが多い。
* [[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] - 長崎県壱岐市（旧芦辺町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * 高下古墳 - 長崎県雲仙市（旧国見町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも言う。 * [[鬼のいわや古墳]] - 熊本県熊本市北区（旧植木町）にある古墳。旧植木町指定史跡。 * [[鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)]] - 熊本県八代市にある古墳。八代市指定史跡。 * [[鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] - 大分県別府市にある古墳。国の史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * [[鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)]] - 宮崎県西都市の[[西都原古墳群]]中の古墳。西都原古墳群は国の特別史跡。西都原206号墳、鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

revision.text:
'''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones.
* {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Iki)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni no Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}
[[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]]

'''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones.
* {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Iki)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni no Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni no Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}

Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya No. 2 Kofun (Beppu)|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}

'''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]][[Category:Kofun disambiguations]] (ancient burial mounds). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones.
* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya No. 2 Kofun (Beppu)|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)|1=Oni-no-iwa Kofun}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}

'''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or [[:en:Oni|Oni]] no Iwa [[Category:Kofun disambiguations]] * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-iwa Kofun|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)|1=Oni-no-iwa Kofun}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."

Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or [[:en:Oni|Oni]] no Iwa [[Category:Kofun disambiguations]] * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-iwa Kofun|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)|1=Oni-no-iwa Kofun}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}

revision.text:
'''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or [[:en:Oni|Oni]] no Iwa
* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=draft}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳|12=draft}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)|12=draft}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-iwa Kofun|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)|ja|鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)|1=Oni-no-iwa Kofun|12=draft}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)|12=draft}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群|12=draft}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."
{{aimai}}
<!--Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun--> <!--Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun-->

revision.text:
'''鬼の岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼ノ岩屋古墳'''、'''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[古墳]]の名称。巨石を使った古墳に対して名づけられることが多い。
* [[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] - 長崎県壱岐市（旧芦辺町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * 高下古墳 - 長崎県雲仙市（旧国見町）にある古墳。長崎県指定史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも言う。 * [[鬼のいわや古墳]] - 熊本県熊本市北区（旧植木町）にある古墳。旧植木町指定史跡。 * [[鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)]] - 熊本県八代市にある古墳。八代市指定史跡。 * [[鬼ノ岩屋古墳 (別府市)]] - 大分県別府市にある古墳。国の史跡。鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。 * [[鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)]] - 宮崎県西都市の[[西都原古墳群]]中の古墳。西都原古墳群は国の特別史跡。西都原206号墳、鬼の岩屋古墳とも表記。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673375]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or [[:en:Oni|Oni]] no Iwa
* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takage Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * [[:en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)]] - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|qq=}} - Located within the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."

* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takage Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * [[:en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)]] - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|qq=}} - Located within the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673375]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or [[:en:Oni|Oni]] no Iwa
* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takage Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * [[:en:Oni-no-iwa Kofun|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)]] - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|qq=}} - Located within the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鬼の岩屋古墳]]
'''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の岩屋古墳), also known as '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' or '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼ノ岩屋古墳, 鬼の窟古墳, おにのいわやこふん), is a name given to [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). This name is often used for kofun constructed using large stones, like a demon moved them, hence the name Demon Stone or {{ill|Oni|en|Oni|WD=Q730052|lt=Oni}} no Iwa
* {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} - Located in Iki City (formerly Ashibe Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Takage Kofun|ja|高下古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takage Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Unzen City (formerly Kunimi Town), Nagasaki Prefecture. A Nagasaki Prefecture-designated historic site. Also called "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Kumamoto City (formerly Ueki Town), Kumamoto Prefecture. A former Ueki Town-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja|鬼の岩屋古墳 (熊本県八代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Yatsushiro)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in Yatsushiro City, Kumamoto Prefecture. A Yatsushiro City-designated historic site. * {{ill|Oni-no-iwa Kofun|en|Oni-no-iwa Kofun|WD=Q11673383|lt=Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Beppu)}} - Located in Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture. A National Historic Site. Also referred to as "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun." * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (西都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Saito)|qq=}} - Located within the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}} in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture. The Saitobaru Kofun Group is designated as a Special Historic Site of Japan. Also referred to as "Saitobaru No. 206 Kofun" or "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun."
{{disambiguation}}
